content.text: Great! I've confirmed the test is failing. Now let me look at the test to understand what's expected:
Now let me look at the Node.java file that contains the bug:

Now I need to understand what the issue is. The failing test `parentlessToString` is testing that when an element is removed from its parent (becomes parentless), its `toString()` method should not include the preceding newline character. Let me look more closely at the test: The test does: 1. Parse a document with an img element 2. Get the img element and confirm that `img.toString()` includes a newline (`\n`) 3. Remove the img from its parent (making it parentless) 4. Check that `img.toString()` no longer includes the newline (``) The issue is likely in how the node's HTML is generated when the node has no parent. Let me look for the `outerHtml` methods and see if there's different behavior based on whether the node has a parent or not. Let me also check if there are any related classes in the Element hierarchy:

content.text:
Let me check the test more carefully. The test expects that when an element has no parent, its toString() should not include the initial newline. Let me look at what `indent()` does if the node has no parent vs. has a parent:

Looking at the code again, the issue appears to be that when an element is removed from its parent, its behavior in `outerHtmlHead` should be different. For an element that has no parent (is parentless), the condition `(parent() != null && parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0)` will be false because `parent()` is null. However, if the element is a block element (`isBlock()`), it will still add the indent.

content.text: Wait, let me re-read the test case and think about the issue again. I think I may have misdiagnosed the problem. Let me run the test again to understand: The test expects: 1. When element HAS parent: `"\n"` (with leading newline) 2. When element has NO parent: `""` (without leading newline) So when the element has a parent, it gets the leading newline. When it's parentless, it should NOT get the leading newline. My fix should work if it's correct. Let me actually double-check what I changed and consider if the issue is elsewhere. Looking at the original code again: ```java void outerHtmlHead(StringBuilder accum, int depth, Document.OutputSettings out) { if (out.prettyPrint() && (isBlock() || (parent() != null && parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0))) indent(accum, depth, out); // ... } ``` The original condition was: `(isBlock() || (parent() != null && parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0))` - This means indent if: element is a block OR (element has parent AND parent can contain block AND element is first sibling) My change was to: `parent() != null && (isBlock() || (parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0))` - This means indent if: (element has parent) AND (element is a block OR (parent can contain block AND element is first sibling)) This should fix the issue, as now parentless elements will never get indented. But let me also consider that the issue might be that `isBlock()` returns true for an img element, which is what's causing the newline when the img is parentless. Actually, looking more carefully, an img tag is typically a self-closing inline element, not a block element. So for an img element, `isBlock()` should return false. The condition in the original code would be: - `(false || (parent() != null && parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0))` - Which reduces to: `(parent() != null && parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0)` So when the img has a parent (which is a body which can contain blocks) and is the first child of its parent, it would get indented. After my change, for a parentless img: - `parent() != null && (isBlock() || (parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0))` - `false && (false || (false))` = `false` - so no indent For a parented img that is first child: - `true && (false || (true && true))` = `true && true` = `true` - so indent This seems correct. Let me run the specific test again, but also make sure it compiles correctly and run some related tests to see if my fix has any side effects: Let me also run all the tests to check if my fix has any side effects: Wait, I noticed that the test is still failing. Let me reconsider the issue. Maybe I should check the original version of the code to see what exactly was causing the problem. Let me revert my change and look at this more methodically:

Let me think about this differently. I need to analyze what condition causes the difference between parented and parentless elements. Let me make the fix again but more carefully:

The original condition: 
`out.prettyPrint() && (isBlock() || (parent() != null && parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0))`

For an img element:
- `isBlock()` is likely false for img tags
- So condition becomes: `out.prettyPrint() && (parent() != null && parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0)`

When img has parent: This will be true if parent can contain blocks and img is first sibling → adds indent
When img has no parent: `parent() != null` is false → no indent will be added

Wait, that seems like it should work already! If `parent() != null` is part of the condition, parentless elements shouldn't get the indent. So why is the test failing?

Unless the issue is that the Document's output settings have pretty printing enabled by default, and the img element is somehow getting indentation even when parentless... Let me reconsider.

Actually wait - I need to be more careful. Let me re-read the test and the condition.

The original condition: `(isBlock() || (parent() != null && parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0))`

If `isBlock()` returns true for img, then the parent condition doesn't matter and it will always get indented, regardless of whether it has a parent.

Let me check if img elements have the isBlock property. Actually, let me just look up if img is a block element (it should be inline), but maybe I need to look at what's going on more carefully.

The bug is that when a node is parentless, we want different behavior. But looking at the original code, the condition should already handle this correctly since it checks `parent() != null` in the second part of the OR.

Actually wait! Let me re-read the condition:
`isBlock() || (parent() != null && parent().tag().canContainBlock() && siblingIndex() == 0)`

If the element is a block (`isBlock()` returns true), then the condition is true regardless of parent status, and it will get indented. If an img element is considered a block, then it gets indented always.

The fix I was thinking about is correct. I need to make it so that the `isBlock()` part only applies when the element has a parent too. This makes sense because a parentless element (standalone) should not be indented based on whether it's a block element.

diff --git a/defects4j.build.properties b/defects4j.build.properties
index ba383387..d5837267 100644
--- a/defects4j.build.properties
+++ b/defects4j.build.properties
@@ -1,8 +1,10 @@
 #File automatically generated by Defects4J
 d4j.bug.id=8
 d4j.classes.modified=org.jsoup.nodes.Node
+d4j.classes.mutate=org.jsoup.nodes.Node
 d4j.classes.relevant=org.jsoup.helper.StringUtil,org.jsoup.helper.Validate,org.jsoup.Jsoup,org.jsoup.nodes.Attribute,org.jsoup.nodes.Attributes,org.jsoup.nodes.Comment,org.jsoup.nodes.DataNode,org.jsoup.nodes.Document,org.jsoup.nodes.Element,org.jsoup.nodes.Entities,org.jsoup.nodes.Evaluator,org.jsoup.nodes.Node,org.jsoup.nodes.TextNode,org.jsoup.nodes.XmlDeclaration,org.jsoup.parser.Parser,org.jsoup.parser.Tag,org.jsoup.parser.TokenQueue,org.jsoup.select.Collector,org.jsoup.select.Elements,org.jsoup.select.NodeTraversor,org.jsoup.select.NodeVisitor,org.jsoup.select.Selector
 d4j.dir.src.classes=src/main/java
 d4j.dir.src.tests=src/test/java
+d4j.major.mutops=AOR,LOR,SOR,COR,ROR,ORU,LVR,STD
 d4j.project.id=Jsoup
 d4j.tests.trigger=org.jsoup.nodes.ElementTest::parentlessToString
diff --git a/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Node.java b/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Node.java
index 084ce124..985e1425 100644
--- a/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Node.java
+++ b/src/main/java/org/jsoup/nodes/Node.java
@@ -360,7 +360,8 @@ public abstract class Node {
     }

     protected void outerHtml(StringBuilder accum) {
-        new NodeTraversor(new OuterHtmlVisitor(accum, ownerDocument().outputSettings())).traverse(this);
+        Document.OutputSettings outputSettings = ownerDocument() != null ? ownerDocument().outputSettings() : (new Document("")).outputSettings();
+        new NodeTraversor(new OuterHtmlVisitor(accum, outputSettings)).traverse(this);
     }

     // if this node has no document (or parent), retrieve the default output settings

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have the proper patch with just the necessary fix. The issue was that when a node becomes parentless (after being removed from its parent), the `ownerDocument()` method returns null, which caused a NullPointerException when trying to call `ownerDocument().outputSettings()`.

The fix handles this case by checking if `ownerDocument()` is null, and if so, creates a new default `Document` instance to get the output settings.

Let me run the test one more time to be sure:

input.description: Final test to confirm the fix works
